reviews.review_description:
Honestly doubted this game when I saw the trailer, 
Wood target's seemed kinda boring but I bought it anyway since there's really nothing to play and it just so good, like the guns, the sounds, the gameplay, it never gets repetitive and it's always a fun experience, I would lovvvee if the devs added more guns, like a micro uzi or old western revolvers, maybe even a lever action?👀 5 star game completely recommend
Fantastic

reviews.review_title: I can’t refund I played for six minutes
reviews.review_description: I want a refund it won’t let me it’s been six minutes and that was my last six bucks

i hope the developers see this i hope y'all add more guns and maybe more rooms? like imagine a dark room where you have to put a flashlight on your gun? it'd be scary but fun that's another thing more weapon attachments imagine being able to add a bigger mag to some guns or even a fast mag i wish there was a revolver on here too i love those pistols oh also imagine being able to buy gear for our character like helmets or vest that'll give us more things to buy! hopefully y'all can add some of this to a new update i really love this game just wish there was more stuff

developer_response.body:
Thanks for checking out Shotwood, and for posting your first ever review here - that's awesome!

More content is planned for this year, and is currently in development. I can't promise specific gear upgrades yet, but the focus is definitely on adding more assessments to master and equipment to play with.

In the meantime though, be sure to check out all of the existing equipment and modes. Have fun in there!

reviews.review_title: What's here is good but it needs more
reviews.review_description: Guns feel nice but I feel the sounds could be a bit punchier and the handling could be refined. It'd be cool to see mag palming added to assist with partial reloads for pistols, smgs, and rifles. Id also just like to see more guns and levels in the future.

reviews.review_title: Pretty much the only thing I play
reviews.review_description: New to VR and was very excited to begin with, now Ive calmed down a bit this game is pretty much all I play. Its an absolute bargain for such a great experience. Yes its a bit dark and depressing but thats the point.
Just perfect, please make more updates soon

This game gets the guns right. It's so satisfying to see the iconic models... 1911, M9, SPAS 12, etc... and get to handle them, reload, add mods like silencer. The whole experience is a tad unnerving as nothing moves except for at the very end. It's so addictive, I 💯 easy and am going thru the harder difficulties. The only thing I wish were different is the secret sidearm... shoulda been a BA revolver or a .50 Desert Eagle. GREAT WORK, CONCRETE BOX!!! Dudes actually very nice, too. Replied to an email I sent gushing about the game.

I honestly didn't think paper targets could be all that complex or entertaining, but I played this one all the way through and then some. I love the weapons and weapon mechanics - they're just about perfect for paper that pops up in front of you and then shoots at you. I like that you can mix things up with the different levels, and I even like the story behind it. It's strangely engaging. I would love some more levels with some more challenges, or some other weapon options. I'm not sure if anything is still in development or not. But there aren't many games out there that are such simple point-and-shoot-and-enjoy-yourself fun. A+
I really enjoyed it, good shooting practice, I recommend it to everyone.

I like the game loop, it's quite addictive. 
Some fresh ideas here and there. Definitely worth checking out

I only wish it had grip toggle, as pressing a button to hold a gun is not realistic or fun (I don't know why VR developers think that it is), and gets very tiring.

I'm hooked on this game every day. I prefer this shooter to all the other shooter games I have. The only issue I have is that when I get the assault rifles scope cross hairs on a distant target(from cover) the bullets often fail to make contact with the targets unless I move out into the open. I use a gunstock, so that may be causing this issue,but the gunstock is the only way for me to take a steady aim. This pistol shooting is very nice! Thanks developers :-)
Its a great game and LOTS OF GUNS which i like but i wish they put more guns and explosives

Highly recommended!

I would not have thought that I would have had this much fun playing a game where the opponents are cardboard cutouts (except, these cardboard cutouts fight back). I also would not have expected to have enjoyed playing a zombie apocalypse game. But it was super immersive, with great ambience, just the right amount of challenge, and lots of great details. Something I looked forward to playing each evening that I could. Lots of hilariously done twists, too. I loved the brick-bat challenge - I was literally laughing all the way through. 

More levels, please!

fun game it’s nice so you can pretend to be like a SWAT team member or something it’s fun you should buy it

￼
A lot of fun, a lot of guns and a lot to shoot! Therapeutic for stress management....

Ok. It’s not 5 stars like say metro awakening, but it’s easy to play, I’m enjoying the h e double hockey sticks out of it.  It has teleport and smooth locomotion, it has a story that takes itself over the top seriously.   The food dispenser is a must stop for sustenance!  I didn’t think shooting “pop-up” targets like this could be fun. Boy was I wrong.  Two big thumbs up from this guy

developer_response.body: Awesome. Thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: Bought the full game after the demo gun play. Mechanics are nice and fun and very stable. I would recommend to anyone.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Shotwood.

reviews.review_description: Just read so many good opinions that played the demo. I like tactical shooting and this is great fun! I like the mysterious ambiance and how it is presented. Just purchased full version,  please keep it updated,  good work! Thanks!

developer_response.body: Awesome. Thanks for playing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: My new fav shooting game! Simple but solid and very entertaining. And chopping balloons is kind of exercise up there with paid fitness exercise apps. Kudos to game producers!

developer_response.body: Awesome. Thanks for checking out Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: I bought this after playing the demo and I’ve spent so much time playing that I feel like the $10 they charge isn’t enough. I’ve spent more time playing this than I have other full price titles.

developer_response.body: Awesome, thanks for checking out Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: Decided to buy this after playing the demo,i was playing this like i’m in a movie dipping behind corners and popping my head out and shooting hehe love this game though.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: This game is very good with the gunplay and the story! Very fun, and the only thing that would make the game nicer is maybe hand physics and the ability to hit stuff with your gun. Other than that, 10/10 game.

developer_response.body: Awesome. Thanks for the feedback, and thanks for checking out Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
Okay, wow. I thought this game was cool and all and had potential just from the demo, but the full game? This game is some of the best ten bucks I've spent in my life (runner-up to Lethal Company!), and for good reason too. The levels are fun, the vibes are great, the visuals are a bit iffy, but it's a $10 dollar game for the Quest 2 so I can't really judge the graphics too hard. The level of accessibility, while still relatively low, still manages to outclass many of the games in my Quest library and even some non-VR games too! (Seriously, being able to adjust my in-game height from directly inside the game speaks VOLUMES to me.)

Pros:

- Visuals are half-decent for a quest game
- The game runs very well, and is probably the only game in my library that doesn't kill the headset's battery (two hours in and my headset still has about half its battery left!)
- Levels are pretty fun and the alternate gamemodes add some level of replayability
- Wow! I can adjust my in-game height!
- Plenty of weapons and tools for all your target practice needs!
- You can flush a toilet!
- You can play with a bouncy ball!
- There's a paintball gun!
- Four slots for save files!
- Soundtrack is alright, not too special but it's decent enough
- You can customize the difficulty to your liking (there's also a permadeath option!)
- Ammo counts for each caliber apply across the entire save file, not just on a per-level basis.
- You can play blackjack
- You can play bootleg Beat Saber
- You can get decked in the skull with a brick

Cons: 

- Recoil is very small and unsatisfying, it's only vertical (from my experience) and stabilizing the gun with your other hand makes recoil a thing of the past
- I know I'm a bad shot but I swear a third of my headshots don't insta-kill
- Not much customization in terms of actual combat and the combat difficulty (no option for moving targets of any sort, no accuracy adjustment, not much indication of defeated targets)
- Weapon sounds are a bit...generic? I've definitely heard some of them in other games both on and off the Unity engine, they're also not very impactful or satisfying in my opinion (Some of them are better than others)
- The practice range is very barebones
- No multiplayer mode(s) :(

I'm not a good gamer nor game reviewer so you'll have to take this review with a grain of salt, this is just my own personal opinion anyway.
In all honesty though, I do think this game is good. It has loads of potential  and, while rough around the edges, still manages to provide a fun experience that I think, given enough time, could turn into something really special. If there's any recommendations I have, it would be to hire a sound designer for just the sound effects, create some sort of multiplayer mode, put more detail and functionality into the game...perhaps even a sandbox mode that can be toggled when creating a new save file and gives you unlimited reserve ammo for all calibers, unlocks all items and weapons and lets you just mess around. Glove skins would be great too. Oh, and don't forget about achievements too, gotta add those. Other than those issues and a few more, I really like this game. I've only had it for a few days as of writing this review, but I'm already having plenty of fun with it right now and I'm sure I'll be having even more in the future. I'm excited to see where this game goes! It makes me sad that it's so unknown.

Anyways, I need to continue the assessments. I hope you see this review and just know that I'm really proud of you!

-JustFrazers2000

Edit: Oh wow, I didn’t actually think you’d get back to me this fast lol 😂 
Glad to hear you’re appreciative of my feedback! I do really love this game and want to see it grow so I wrote this review, and I’m happy to know you actually read reviews. Sorry if mine was a bit long, again I’m not good at reviewing games. I would like to know if you have a Discord server by any chance. Not that I’ll be an active member or anything, but I would at least like to know. Also, for the recoil system, I would recommend something like BONEWORKS, where it’s very quick and snappy, just powerful enough to add to the experience but not too much to where it makes aiming a chore. I also checked out your website so knowing now that your “team” is just you, I don’t want you to worry about rushing any updates or even giving any specific dates, they’re just changes I would like to eventually see. :)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for checking out Shotwood, and for posting such a detailed review. This is great feedback, and it's really encouraging to hear.

Your recommendations have been noted and will be considered for future updates, though I can’t promise any specific features just yet. I do really like the sandbox mode idea though – that’s a really interesting concept, and great food for thought.

Thanks for playing Shotwood, and have fun continuing the assessments!

reviews.review_description: Like other reviewers, I find myself mystified as to why I didn’t buy this eons ago.  It’s fab meta fun.  And yes, addictive!

developer_response.body: Thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: This game has great gun mechanics, an interesting story (more lore please - hidden books or clues as to what's going on) and has great replay value. While a little more polish around the edges may help (load times, more info, higher resolution targets, checkpoints), what matters here most is the gun play and this game has that dialed in. There was a large, free update recently, and I'm looking forward to more. I agree with the previous review from Pow. Keep up the great work! Give this game a go if you like gun play mechanics.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great feedback and suggestions. Checkpoints are already available to use - these were added in as part of the large Tactical Toolset update, they can be enabled/disabled by changing the Respawn Location in the Difficulty settings (through the options tablet). Thanks for playing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: I picked up the demo a few days ago and have been playing every night after work. Decided today to buy the full game. Great game for killing time and giving your reflexes a workout. I highly recommend picking this up if you are looking for something to fill the void before all the new upcoming games this year. Keep up the great work!

developer_response.body: Awesome. Thanks for checking out Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
I wasn’t expecting much from this game going in, seeing as how the graphics are a little bland, and the bad guys are just cardboard cutouts… but was I WAY WRONG!  There’s no other way for me to describe it than to say Shotwood ADDICTING AS HECK! I found myself playing the same level over & over & over to achieve the goal/requirement. This game NEEDS to be on the Main Oculus Store… this would be a Top 5 Seller within 1 month of release. 

PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE 
 - Add more guns
 - Add more levels
 - Add more goals
 - Basically, just more of everything haha. I would gladly pay extra for some DLC. You sir, deserve every penny

Update 5/30/22: I wanted to add 2 small suggestions to my review… 
#1- It can be annoying not having any way to check your current stats out. The only way to see your stats is to complete the level. It’d be helpful to know if I already failed a particular Mission/Objective. For instance, maybe adding how many times I’ve been Hit underneath the ammo count on my wrist. I hate completing a mission thinking i haven’t been hit only to find out i was hit once and have to redo the entire level. 
Or adding how many friendlies I’ve killed so far…Basically, this is a long way of saying I hate having to wait until I finish a level to learn my statistics. Please add some in Level info markers. 

#2- It would be incredibly helpful and time saving if you added a “Restart Level” feature. It sucks dying and ending up back in the lobby, only to have to take the elevator back down to the level. Just seems like a bunch of wasted time. 

Please keep up the great work! Love seeing developers respond to the customer reviews and you do that better than anyone else. Kudos

UPDATE 12/30/22: 
GREAT update. Hope you keep ‘em comin :) more areas/levels is what’s desperately needed. And maybe some more handguns. Love everything your doing with the longarm firearms. There’s truly no better shotgun mechanics in any other game I’ve played. That’s somethin to be proud of since there are tons of FPS’s on Quest 2. Wish every FPS would implement your gun mechanics.


FINAL UPDATE 8/18/24
Just wanted to say it’s a dang shame this game hasn’t sold better… 50+ reviews, 4.9 score, and 95% are glowing 5 star reviews, and yet this game still gets no love. This is a special dev. Love the way he keeps updating this gem.  When I first bought the game i thought it was 10/10… now with everything added in the last 2 years, it’s the perfect shooter. I can’t say enough good things about the physics… No game comes close to the actual feeling of holding/handling a firearm like ShotWood achieves.  
Thanks for all the updates… Everything has been added that I could dream of, but you’ve made the game so dang good it now feels ripe for a Co-Op mode of some sort… where my opponent watches my run, then vice versa, then compare scores after 3 rounds. That’s an incredibly big ask though so I ain’t gonna hold it against ya if ya say “heck no” lol.

REALLY REALLY FINAL UPDATE 3/26
I’m pretty bummed this game hasn’t sold like gangbusters but man do I appreciate you still updating this hidden gem.  You’re a great dev. If you ever do a kickstarter for a new game, I’ll be first to donate. Keep up the great work and I can’t wait to see what the future holds for this game. THANK YOU for not abandoning this project. You have the best shooting physics I’ve ever experienced in VR, and I promise, I’ve played em all.

developer_response.body:
It's great to hear that you're still enjoying the game. Thank you so much for your continued support, and for the additional feedback and suggestions. 

Asynchronous multiplayer is a really interesting idea, it could work great in Shotwood. It is a ton of work to do this properly though, as any multiplayer features would greatly complicate the project overall. 

That said, I won't rule this out for the future. If Shotwood experiences a surge of popularity, this (and more cool stuff) may become possible. However, it's looking unlikely at the moment. 

I hope you continue to enjoy the core game into the future though, and thanks again for sticking around.

reviews.review_description: **A Hidden Gem.** At first glance, it might seem like there's nothing exciting about shooting cardboard targets. But trust me, things here are not as simple as they seem—it's incredibly engaging and full of challenges.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: It’s not letting me past the second mission

developer_response.body:
Hi there.

If you're seeing "Check Loadout" in the elevator when trying to descend to Assessment B1, make sure you've selected a shotgun from the equipment terminal then try again.

Most assessments (after A2) have weapon-class requirements. These are shown on the information panel, behind the level select buttons. You're still free to use your sidearm or other weapons in any assessment, but you must have at least one weapon of the listed class selected to access those floors.

I hope this helps. If you're still unable to progress, or if you have any other questions, please email concreteboxvr@gmail.com so we can look into it further and get you back on track.

Thanks for playing Shotwood, and have fun!

reviews.review_description:
While looking for a game similar to H3VR on PC, I came across this hidden gem of a game. While it's not H3VR, it offers a unique gameplay experience that I was looking for, which is a shoot-house base game. The game has you running thru shoot-house style stages where you shot down wooden target/enemies that will also shoot back at you. This requires you to take cover or react fast as there is a split second delay when they attack once they see you. There are also friendly targets to avoid & explosives that can be set off. While there are only 8 stages, each are highly replayable. The game offers several challenges that you can complete on a run including completing the stage in a set time limit, breaking all targets, headshots only or avoid getting hit. Aside from the challenges, the game offers two additional game modes for each stage. Both modes randomizes the target & makes other changes to how the stage is played out, making each run unique & fresh. Speed running thru each stage is very enjoyable.

One thing I didn't expect, especially for a small indie game like this, was the story & game lore. It has a similar Portal-like story where you are a test subject within a facility task to do various test or assessments. As you progress, you learn more about the facility and what's actually going on behind the scenes. Similarly, there is a narrator/overseer that communicates with you as you play thru the game. What I enjoyed most about this was the random announcement or fun facts it would make about the facility & game lore. There's a lot of them & they are cleverly written. Limitations in the game itself (such as non-interactable objects) are explained & rationalized in clever ways. I enjoyed the random explanations it would make for certain objects in the game (ie toilet paper rolls). Good writing overall.

One thing I would recommend when playing the game is setting the in-game radio to the festive holiday music. Fits the game light humor tone quite well.

developer_response.body: Awesome! Thank you for reviewing Shotwood.

reviews.review_description:
shotwood is a great game but i only have one big issue, the thing is i shoot left handed and i move with the left joystick but you guys set the default to left handed mode to moving with the right joystick, if you guys add a seperate setting to switch the movement options around for comfort it would be great. 

i love your game and this is the only gamebreaking issue to me

edit: this is fixed in the new update thanks very much

developer_response.body: You're welcome. Thanks for sticking around, and thanks for rating Shotwood. Have fun in there!

reviews.review_description:
The game itself is very good. I finished all assessments and it would not unlock the last four rifles. The gambling RNG is very bad and I suspect the developer coded the game so the player loses more often than not. I tracked the gambling game, which cards were dealt to me and the dealer. I also tracked the number of face cards and frequency which they appeared, and statistically there is no way that they dealer could be winning so many times with out the help of some pre programmed  algorithm. So one star if it gets fixed, if there is going to ever be another update.

I am updating the review to three stars, because the dev did respond. I did unlock the new guns by starting a new game plus. I have experience playing black jack, so I know how to play the game. It just seems to me that Face cards are drawn more often than not. There will be times that the dealer just so coincidently, gets face cards and beats my by one or two. I quite playing the simplejack. 
I really like the game, I just do not think the RNG is be randomized enough for the black jack game.

Update: Came back to the game after the update and guns are now unlocking. Also now I understand that Black Jack game is using one deck.  Updated to 5 stars. Love the new update.

developer_response.body:
Hi there.

It's great to hear that you find the game overall to be very good.

Regarding rifle unlocks - this has been fixed.

There was one very rare bug discovered in the update system of the previous version of the game - v1021. This has been fixed in v1047 with the Tactical Toolset update.

This bug affected a very small number of legacy savestates, when upgrading to v1021 from even earlier versions. The update system failed to unlock the newly-added weapons for a very small subset of players, if their savestate had very specific criteria in it.

In all cases that this was reported to us, via support email, we manually patched users save files to ensure they could continue without starting a new game. The issue was then thoroughly investigated and fixed. We've even added an extra savestate validation tool to the game (to automatically detect missing unlocks and fix them, every time the game starts) to ensure it can not happen again.

All weapons should reliably unlock when upgrading from any previous version of the game. There should never be any weapons that cannot unlock. Without exceptions. Please see the Tactical Toolset update developer post for full details.

We've also added unlock tips to the equipment terminal, to better communicate when/how each weapon should unlock. In several cases we received reports that weapons did not unlock, when the unlock criteria simply had not yet been met (in those cases it wasn't a bug).

In the very unlikely event that you are still experiencing unlock issues, please email our support address - concreteboxvr@gmail.com - so we can investigate it and get you up and running. Feel free to reach out with general questions also, we are happy to help!

Regarding the SimpleJack gambling minigame - the code was fully reviewed during the most recent update, and I can confirm that there is nothing in there that gives the dealer any advantage. It deals completely randomly, from a standard deck of 52 cards - of which 16 are face cards & aces (almost a third).

It's expected to see at least one face card or ace per game (4+ cards are dealt). But, because the deal is fully random, it's possible that every card dealt is a face card, or none at all. The code that deals the cards is blind to the type of card, it just picks a random slot in the deck and deals card from it.

Sadly not possible to make this code any more random than it already is, there really isn't anything happening in there that chooses cards by value. It's fully and completely random.

It is maximum randomness.

That said, it has been noted that this minigame can feel overly punishing at times, with bad luck streaks having no cap on the amount of in-game currency the player can lose, yet the dealer only has limited funds to win (so good luck streaks can't run). I will consider possible ways to reduce the frustration this minigame can cause, as Shotwood aims to be a fun experience and these frustrating moments can really ruin that.

SimpleJack does have a free mode, in which you can play without risking any credits. Also, as of the most recent update, the SimpleJack minigame is now completely optional. It's possible to fully 100% complete Shotwood ever without playing this minigame. The shooting challenges, various modes, and other minigames are a much more dependable (and often more enjoyable) way to earn credits in the game.

Thank you for playing Shotwood, and have fun in there!

reviews.review_description:
#1 favorite game. If I have any complaints it's only that I want more content, love this game! Great Job!
Can't wait to get home and see the new stuff!! I just think this game is still one of the best! If we could combine In2 the Radius/or Deus ex Machina and Shotwood we wouldn't need any more games developed!! Thank you so much for all your hard work!!

UPDATE: I just read the review of the blackjack game being rigged and I will admit it has felt like that, I must say that I have always been able to win all the money with a little persistence. I don't know if double is supposed to split the hand into two but small bets or just keep earning money on the shooting floor.

developer_response.body:
That's really awesome to hear. Have a blast with this new update! Thanks for playing Shotwood.

Update - regarding the in-game-currency gambling mechanic:

SimpleJack is a simplified digital black-jack themed minigame. The "double" feature is one of the simplifications. It will never split the hand. Instead it doubles the wager, hits, and stands all in one move.

I've fully reviewed the card-dealing code again, during the recent update. I can confirm there is no intentional rigging in there. This code is super simple and efficient - it keeps a list of 52 cards, uses a random number generator to pick an index (a slot in that list), then it pulls out whatever card is in that slot, deals it, and repeats. It really is fully random, there's nothing complex happening in there.

Of course, being fully random means sometimes it just deals bad cards. Sometimes even many times in a row. It never does this intentionally, it just happens. I did consider overhauling it to try to produce fake wins for the player, but honestly this just felt wrong to me. I've decided to keep it random.

However, this minigame is the sole reason Shotwood has such a high age classification, which hurts the entire game as a whole. I may consider swapping out SimpleJack for something else in the future, if it continues to be a source of frustration for players. Though, honestly it would be a real shame to lose this classic minigame, and it also feels extremely wrong to remove features that players may be enjoying. I really don't want to do that.

In any case, your advice is spot-on; small wagers are the way to go when playing SimpleJack. I also find that standing at 16 can be a good additional strategy, as the dealer cannot hit their own hand in SimpleJack. Two cards are all they get. The dealer is more likely to have a low score because of that, going bust can be a bigger risk than standing low.

As of the most recent update, it's possible to fully 100% complete Shotwood without ever engaging with this minigame. It's completely optional at this point. So you're absolutely right, certainly sticking to the shootings floors (challenges, modes, or other minigames) is a much more reliable way to progress through the game!

reviews.review_title: Best single player shooter on Oculus
reviews.review_description: I love just about everything in this game. The eerie feeling like I'm being watched, the fantastic weapons mechanics, the bundles of cash well hidden around the maps, and the suspicious looking locked door downstairs. I'm trying to to find a way to bring the paintball gun with me to a range and replace a handgun with it to bring it with me back to my "room." Extremely underrated game and trying to 100%

developer_response.body: It's great to hear you're enjoying the game. Thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
Donot u think it's so weird that shooting the gun without any reaction in the VR game? Why not add the handle vibration? That will make this game much better I SWEAR！！
And then plz do not stop updating Im very very excited to shoot more WWII or more old gun in ur game even if that's only in DLC I will be very glad to pay for it

🎁Shotwood 25％ off🎁
tiny.cc/shotwood25

Copy and paste the link above into the internet address bar and click "Accept" before purchasing!

developer_response.body:
That's awesome, thank you! All future content for Shotwood will be free.

Shotwood currently does have a setting for haptics in the options tablet, though it is turned off by default as this feature still requires a bit of polish. I will bump this up the priority list though!

Thanks again for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
This game has such great mechanics but lacks content. I'm pretty sure that if this had a level editor it would be much more popular. The weapons feel so good to handle and the shooting in general is so satisfying while also being challengin.
So buy this game! Even the few existing levels are worth your time and have multiple challenges so you have plenty to do.

developer_response.body:
Hi there.

A level editor would be an awesome feature, and it's actually one that has been on the internal development wish-list for a long time. However, this feature would pose some significant technical/performance challenges due to how Shotwood's engine setup handles lighting, occlusion, collision, and other under-the-hood stuff.

This could mean that a level editor would be a very restricted feature, potentially much lower in quality than the existing levels, and that's something I'd like to avoid with Shotwood.

For these reasons, it hasn't made it past the wish-list yet. I won't rule it out for the future, but for now the focus is on improving the overall experience and adding more general content to the game.

The next update - the Tactical Toolset update - is currently in the final testing stages and will be out very, very soon! Check out the Concrete Box social media links for a short preview of the new tools coming in this update. This will add more variety to the gameplay, as well as a brand new mode, and some other neat features.

Thanks for playing Shotwood, and for posting a great review!

reviews.review_title: Good pistol and rifle VR training, BUT..
reviews.review_description:
Pro: 

-Realistic weapons and handling.
 
-Flicker-free sharp graphics 

-Many stages 

-A Paintball Airsoft Pistol :-D



Cons: 

-Weapons could shine a little more

- Gunshot noise a bit too low

-The life bar above the target is out of place and interferes with the immersion of a target range. 
After a headshot, the targets should disappear directly into the ground and not only the bar should turn red and the target should disappear 3 seconds later. 
That kind of slows the whole thing down. In Gunclub shooting is more fun because only one or 2 targets can be seen at the same time, and not 10 or more in one room, as is sometimes the case here. 

In short, dead targets should disappear faster and it would be more fun if targets gradually rise like in gunclub.

Still a nice change for a good price for fans of Gunclub vr.

developer_response.body:
Hi there.

I'd like to overhaul the weapon visuals and sounds, though this is a very costly process so it's dependent on how popular Shotwood proves to be. It's on the development wishlist, but the current roadmap is focussed on improving the core gameplay then adding more general content.

I will be expanding the options tablet a little, as part of those improvements. I will explore the idea of making target life-bars optional, and having targets fall immediately when defeated if the life-bar is disabled. If this proves functional / feasible, this option will become available in the near future!

Thank you for the feedback, and thanks for playing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
I really like this game. It makes you think a little, the gameplay is satisfying and the narrative is great, it made me smile a few times. Not a stressful game play either, and finally for the price it’s worth a go.

Update… 
This game has been updated so I jumped back in to play. I had forgotten how good it is. This is such fun to play. I cannot wait to try out the new guns. Maybe a developer post to fully explain the update would be good. 👍👍

developer_response.body: That's a great suggestion, thank you. I'll look into getting a developer post up as soon as possible. For now, please see our website (www.concretebox.net) for more info on this update. Thanks for rating Shotwood, and have fun!

reviews.review_description: It’s a great game, it’s one of my favourites, but how do I do the beyond the booth, gone there, but I can’t figure out how to progress past the headphones room

developer_response.body:
Thanks for rating Shotwood!

MINOR SPOILERS: Be sure to select some weapons before travelling to this area. Next to the headphones you will find a small keycard, this can be used on one of the locked doors in the area. Inside that room, there is a panel which will allow you to unlock another door. Inside the next room you'll find your selected weapons. You can use these to blast open some panels above the door at the end of the hallway!

I hope this helps. If you have any other questions feel free to contact me directly at: concreteboxvr@gmail.com

Thanks for playing Shotwood, have fun!

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoy playing this game has loads of relatability. This isn't a first person shooter in the traditional sense, this is far more tactical and strategic. 
Have played all the missions so far, but the challenges keeps bringing me back for more. Some of the challenges are quite basic, while the tougher ones require a bit more thought and time.
Blitz mode lets you put that training to the test, seeing how fast and efficiently you can clear a random array of targets and locations 
Also hats off to Ben, who I emailed as I couldn't find a target I needed to eliminate. Who kindly advised where they might be.

developer_response.body: I'm glad we could help you find that one - feel free to get back in touch if you need any other tips & tricks. Thanks for reviewing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: Gets you to think in a much better way than other fps games, amazing!

developer_response.body: Thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: I've never written a review, at least I don't think so. I do smoke a lot of weed. This game is stupid fun. It's not perfect of course. It's not the best looking game either. We all know that award goes to everything shown in the masterpiece we called The lawnmower man. But the mechanics are great and I have a wonderful emerged time playing it. Props 2 the developers! I want a good future for them. Thanks again guys for all the hard work and gosh darnit I  love VR it's the best thing ever

developer_response.body: Thanks!

reviews.review_description: For one person to come up with this game and deliver an incredible fun experience is amazing. Very realistic gun dynamics, silky smooth and glitch free mixed with great humour and topped with excitement and originality. I really hope we get to see more. What are you waiting for?! Buy it and support this developer! Love it!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_title: Way more fun than Gun Club!!!
reviews.review_description: Very addicting gameplay and great killhouse scenarios! Gun Club was fun and all but something about being able to actually move around and have these "scenarios" is fantastic. Would be great if eventually the targets could rock side to side or glide on the ground like on a moving track just to add even more variety. Even still as is I can't stop jumping in again and again and pretending I'm cool. :)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback!

reviews.review_description:
This is an excellent tactical CQB sim where you can practice solo clearing rooms. While the enemies don't move, they do shoot so it's vital to take cover, making it a physical game as well.

I like tactical shooters and this fills that niche very nicely. It's exactly what I would have wanted from a H3VR on Quest, with focus on fun QCB gameplay instead of hyper-realistic gun handling.

Highly recommended for us tactically minded players!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review!

reviews.review_description:
Really great, very immersive, great fun, excellent graphics and great gun mechanics. Also funny commentary. 

Been through all the levels and unlocked the mysterious door. Was not expecting that. 👍🏻

Highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Thanks for leaving a review!

reviews.review_title: Good, but more lefty options needed..
reviews.review_description:
I'm leaving my first impressions here as I have to address some lefty options that are sorely needed. Regarding locomotion. When you switch to left-handed mode. The game swaps the locomotion sticks around. That's fine for a few select lefty players. But in most cases left handed players play with locomotion hand on the left and look/snap turn on right. A button on the tablet that says swap locomotion hand will solve this issue. The game is very playable with both hands when in the right-handed mode. So I'll use that until it's updated.

Now, the game. Bare in mind this is only my first impressions of the game. But I have to say it feels and looks very polished. Guns feel smooth, reloading is great, love that you have an animation for clip in clip out, then rigid body physics on the mags when dropped etc. Would've loved some in world collision when holding guns but I guess that hurts performance. Like zero cal has. Combat is different, from other target based shooter games, enemies don't suddenly fall but rather stay a while until they fall down. I'm guessing this a choice made for more accurately displaying their health. But I found myself shooting alot more, at least on the targets far away. Couldn't really tell if they we're dead or not 😅 bu i quickly got used to looking at the health bar and not paying attention to the enemy itself. 

Love the progression system, level design and overall finish, can't wait to save up enough money to get the automatic weapons. The atmosphere and the announcervoice is a great touch. Also cool that you added the BlackJack Minigame 😁 oh, and i absolutely recommend this, having almost played every shooter there is on quest.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. I've added this to the to-do list, a movement-hand option will be added in a future update!

reviews.review_description: This would be my favorite game if the was a mode similar to take and hold from h3vr i begg with tears in my eyes make it a thing pleassee.!!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the positive feedback and request - I've made a note of it.

While I can't promise that exact one, I would definitely like to make more modes for the game in the future - adding interesting new twists to the assessment floors.

Updates will be announced on Facebook and Twitter (@ConcreteBoxVR) so be sure to follow us there to stay up to date!

reviews.review_description: Sandbox is fun like we use the guns to spawn enemies and we could spawn guns￼￼￼￼

developer_response.body: I can't promise a sandbox mode yet, but I will certainly explore the idea. Thanks for the request!

reviews.review_description: I love this game, I have actually done shoothouse runs, and this is good. If you make another one, let me know! I reset everyday and run through it all over again. I do recommend this game.

developer_response.body: It's awesome to hear that you're enjoying Shotwood, and replaying it over and over! There's no sequel planned as of yet, but more updates are on the way - including additional content. Thanks for playing, and thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description:
Man, this game is just fun. The gun physics feel really good. 

The game has an almost Portal-like feel as you are being used as some kind of test subject, but instead of getting a Portal gun, you get real guns!

The Hogans-Alley like feel of shooting wooden cut-outs that can sometimes shoot back is great.

The game feels like it should get boring pretty quick, but it just doesn’t.

Buy this game. It is well worth the money!

developer_response.body: Thanks for reviewing Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: Been playing this for a few days now, and this is the first game that gets the pump action shotgun reloading spot on. The logistics are very good and having problems with my left arm it's very good I can play at my own pace. My only gripe is I wish you could check your ammo in the guns.... Unless I'm just being dim. Highly recommended if you like fun club type games.... And arguably better😁

developer_response.body:
Thanks for posting this great review!

You're correct, there is no ammo counter for the guns themselves, only for the ammo in your pouch - this is shown on your smartwatch when you're holding the weapon in either hand.

Most guns in Shotwood can be "press-checked" - you can pull the slide or bolt back slightly to visually inspect the chamber for a cartridge. You can also look down into a magazine to see if has any rounds remaining.

I hope that helps for now. I will consider adding an option (or perhaps a weapon attachment) that shows the number of rounds loaded.

Thanks for checking out Shotwood.

reviews.review_description:
the collision with the wall is weird. Usually, when I move toward wall, I expect to move as much as possible, even if it's as slow. But, it stops at all. just stick to the wall. when I have to run away in an emergency, I stick to the wall, and even the screen turns black because of the anti-cheat. really annoying.
Also, the price of ammunition is too high, and the basic reward of assessments is too low. Of course, I can earn money by playing mini-games, but the players in this game would want to just shoot without worrying about money and wouldn't expect to burst balloons with knives for the very sad reason that they're short of money.

developer_response.body:
Hi there. 

Thanks for checking out Shotwood, it's great to hear that you're enjoying the game overall. 

Regarding the movement system; the game does prevent movement towards any wall that your body is in contact with, but you should still be able to move away from it without sticking. If your head passes through a wall (or window) the system will wait for you to lean/step back through. If your head passes into a solid object it will also black out to prevent you seeing straight through the map, as this can be disorienting and extremely immersion-breaking. 

For the best experience, it can help to keep at least a small distance away from the walls where possible. If you do find yourself stuck to a wall, try to lean very slightly away, this should free you up immediately. 

Thanks for your feedback regarding the ammo cost and rewards. I will may explore the idea of boosting the base assessment reward in a future update, though I will need to check how this effects the wider game balancing. 

Shotwood is currently balanced to encourage engagement with the challenge system. Challenge rewards range from 50 to 500 credits each (depending on the difficulty) on top of the base reward. Multiple challenges can be beat on a single run, so they make a great way to get a lot of extra credits quickly. 

You can also earn up to 500 credits per run in Blitz mode (depending on your grade) in just a few minutes. Additionally, you can find cash wads hidden throughout the facility. There is also a new game plus feature built into the grievance booth. When using the booth to start over, you will begin with 250 credits for every percentage point complete in your previous game! 

I'll be looking into adding more ways to gain additional credits too, as well as possibly some new weapons to purchase with them, later in the year. 

I hope this helps anyway. Thanks again for checking out Shotwood, and for rating and reviewing. 

Have fun!

reviews.review_description:
The game is interesting.  Gunclub mix with portal brushstrokes.  They have to improve the sounds of the weapons (do the guns sound the same?) and above all the collisions with the guns and the weapons are non-existent.  But still, it is very well optimized and fun.
Please, subtitles in Spanish.

developer_response.body: Hi there. All 20+ guns do have individual / different sounds for firing, reloading, and so on, but you're right - some of them can seem similar at times. Shotwood is no longer in early access as of the recent Tactical Toolset update, it is now a fully complete experience. I will add Spanish subtitles to the requested features list though. If localization is possible in the future, this option may become available! Thanks for rating Shotwood.

reviews.review_description:
It’s my favorite app lab game but it would be rated higher if I could grab my shotgun from my back. No matter how hard I tried I can’t grab my shotgun from my back
(Edit) I figured out how to grab the shotgun and I’ve progressed a lot in this game and I’m happy to say that this and historical crafts are my two favorite app lab games. Thanks dev

developer_response.body:
I've taken another look at the grab-collider placement for this, after reading your review. I can see that there is definitely some room for improvement here. I'll increase the size of this zone in a future update, to make this much easier.

For now, the best technique for retrieving a longarm from your back, is to reach your hand slightly past your ear (close to your head) as though you're grabbing the back of your head / neck.

I hope this helps. If you need any further support feel free to contact concreteboxvr@gmail.com directly.

Thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: Can't wait to see where it goes

developer_response.body: Awesome, thanks for rating Shotwood!

reviews.review_description: This game is absolutely incredible, especially for a 1-man studio! The guns sound and feel great, the levels and main hub have an eery feeling that reminds me of aperture from the portal series, the side content is a good way te escape from the main missions and thanks to blitz-mode the game also has a good amount of replayabilty! I definitely recommend it!

developer_response.body: That's great to hear, thanks for posting a review!

reviews.review_description: The firearms in this game have a good feel. It's a ton of fun and is better than Gun Club. The only issue is that sometimes I get stuck on the doorway/wall and have to move the joystick back for a couple seconds to free myself. The biggest annoyance with that is that the white walls are so bright it's like a flashbang.

developer_response.body:
That's good to hear! 

Regarding the movement issues - currently, the locomotion systems lock while the headset is colliding with a virtual wall or leaning through a virtual window. If you find yourself stuck, try moving your head very slightly away from the virtual wall or window, the locomotion systems should unlock immediately, allowing you to turn or move away. 

The game also prevents locomotion in the direction of a wall, when you are extremely close to it (almost touching it). This is by design, as the physics engine is not used at all for the locomotion systems! This is on my internal "known issues" list though, as it's a fairly clunky solution, and it too can make you feel as though you are getting stuck on a wall. But, in this case, you can turn or move away immediately, as the locomotion system remains fully active. 

I hope to explore ways to potentially improve this, for a future update.

Thanks for posting a review!

reviews.review_description:
If you like Gun Club VR I highly recommend you try this one! 

To be honest I bought the game without much expectations but came out quite surprised. I played multiple vr titles similar to this with only two having a permanent seat in my library, and this game is one of the two! 
I'm addicted to this game wether it being the gunplay/guns that are realistic, smooth and satisfying to use, with some really good gun models to top it off or the progress slowing addiction I have to the SimpleJack mini game. 

It really surprised me just how much I ended up liking the game, I thought Gun Club Vr was going to be my go-to, let loose and shoot some guns game, but this one just won the race by miles. 

I remember seeing somewhere that this game was being developed by one maybe two people? Don't quote me on that, whether or not that is the case I believe the developer(s) have made an impressive game in my opinion that is better than any of the other shooting range vr games out right now!

developer_response.body: Awesome, it's great to hear that you're enjoying Shotwood. Thanks for checking it out, and for rating and reviewing!
